title: Bio-Rad iTaq PCR
description: Bio-Rad iTaq DNA Polymerase: 50 uL antibody hot-start reaction requiring a 95 C / 3 min activation.

steps.name: Extension
steps.duration: 30 sec to several min (scale to amplicon)

payload.notes: Vendor: Bio-Rad, iTaq DNA Polymerase (Cat. 170-8870). Antibody-mediated hot start: the 95 C / 3 min activation step is mandatory to release active Taq. Extension time is scaled to product length (the manual gives 30 sec to several minutes; no per-kb rate is published, so set it from your amplicon). The manual cycling table has no separate final-extension step. Annealing 55 C is the manual's example, optimize to your primer Tm. Cycles 25-40. Primers 100-500 nM. MgCl2 1.5 mM (separate 50 mM stock supplied for optimization). Source: https://www.bio-rad.com/webroot/web/pdf/lsr/literature/4106202B.pdf
